skill.name: application-performance-performance-optimization
skill.description: Optimize end-to-end application performance with profiling, observability, backend tuning, frontend tuning, load testing, and monitoring guardrails.
skill.summary: Coordinate measurable performance optimization across application, database, frontend, infrastructure, and observability layers.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns and system reconnaissance, but the reviewed SKILL.md content is documentation for performance workflows. The crypto findings are false positives caused by ordinary performance and markdown text, and the reconnaissance finding is a safety warning about load testing production. No malicious intent, prompt injection, code execution, secret access, or exfiltration behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported weak cryptographic algorithm indicators at SKILL.md lines 3, 14, 77, and 109. These lines describe performance optimization, load tests, distributed systems, and load testing tools. They do not contain cryptographic code, hash functions, encryption settings, or instructions to use weak algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are plain workflow text and do not reference cryptographic primitives or security configuration. The nearby context confirms they are performance planning instructions.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported system reconnaissance at SKILL.md line 32. The line tells users to avoid load testing production without approvals and safeguards, which is a protective operational warning rather than reconnaissance behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line discourages unsafe production load testing and contains no commands, host discovery steps, or data collection instructions. The semantic context is safety guidance.

low_findings.title: Operational Caution: Performance Testing Can Affect Systems
low_findings.description: The skill includes load testing and production monitoring workflows. These activities are legitimate for performance optimization, but users should apply approvals, rate limits, test environments, and rollback plans before running high-load tests or production changes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly covers load testing and monitoring setup, which can affect live services if misapplied. The risk is operational rather than malicious because the file also includes safety guidance.

content.user_title: Optimize Application Performance End to End
content.value_statement: Performance problems often span code, databases, frontend delivery, infrastructure, and monitoring. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through a structured optimization workflow with measurable baselines and validation.
actual_capabilities: Defines a phased workflow for profiling, baselines, optimization, validation, and monitoring.
Covers backend, database, frontend, CDN, mobile, and distributed system performance topics.
Recommends metrics such as response times, Core Web Vitals, throughput, error rates, and resource use.
Guides load testing with realistic traffic scenarios and regression testing in CI/CD.
Supports observability planning with tracing, dashboards, alerts, SLIs, SLOs, and runbooks.
Includes configuration options for performance focus, optimization depth, tooling, budgets, and rollout tolerance.
limitations: It provides workflow guidance and prompts, not built-in profiling tools or automated measurements.
It depends on access to reliable metrics, traces, logs, and representative traffic patterns.
It cannot guarantee performance gains without implementation, testing, and production validation.
Load testing and infrastructure changes require user approval and environment-specific safeguards.

use_cases.title: Diagnose Slow Critical Journeys
use_cases.description: Establish baselines, identify bottlenecks, and prioritize fixes for important user paths.

use_cases.title: Prepare For Traffic Growth
use_cases.description: Plan load tests, capacity targets, scaling strategies, and monitoring before a launch.

use_cases.title: Improve Frontend Experience
use_cases.description: Analyze Core Web Vitals, bundle size, loading behavior, mobile responsiveness, and CDN delivery.

prompt_templates.title: Start A Performance Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review performance for my application. Focus on current symptoms, available metrics, critical user journeys, and the first baseline measurements we need.

prompt_templates.title: Find Bottlenecks From Metrics
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to analyze these performance metrics and traces. Identify likely bottlenecks, rank them by user impact, and recommend measurable next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Full Stack Optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create an optimization plan across database, backend, frontend, CDN, and observability. Include expected metrics, validation tests, and rollout controls.

prompt_templates.title: Design Enterprise Performance Program
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a continuous performance program with budgets, load testing, regression tests, SLOs, dashboards, alerts, capacity planning, and ownership.

output_examples.input: Our checkout page is slow during peak traffic.
output: A baseline plan for checkout latency, error rate, database query time, and frontend load metrics.
A prioritized bottleneck list covering slow queries, API calls, bundle weight, and third-party scripts.
A validation plan with load tests, rollout checkpoints, and rollback criteria.

output_examples.input: We need to support twice the current peak load next quarter.
output: A capacity planning outline using current throughput, resource utilization, and growth assumptions.
A load testing strategy for normal, peak, and stress scenarios.
Monitoring recommendations for saturation, latency, error budgets, and business-critical flows.

output_examples.input: Our mobile Core Web Vitals are failing.
output: A frontend diagnosis plan for LCP, interaction delay, layout shift, JavaScript cost, and asset delivery.
Optimization steps for lazy loading, critical rendering, image formats, and mobile CPU constraints.
A regression guardrail plan using performance budgets and automated checks.

best_practices: Set target metrics before changing code, infrastructure, or caching behavior.
Test high-load scenarios in controlled environments before applying production traffic.
Validate each optimization with before-and-after measurements and rollback criteria.
anti_patterns: Optimizing without baselines, profiling data, or user impact ranking.
Running production load tests without approvals, safeguards, or monitoring.
Treating one performance metric as success while ignoring errors, cost, and user experience.

faq.question: Does this skill run performance tests automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides a structured workflow and prompts for planning, executing, and validating performance work.

faq.question: What data should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide metrics, traces, logs, traffic patterns, user journeys, architecture details, and recent performance changes.

faq.question: Can it help with frontend and backend issues?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers frontend, backend, database, CDN, mobile, distributed systems, and observability concerns.

faq.question: Is it safe for production systems?
faq.answer: The guidance includes safeguards, but production testing and changes still require approvals, limits, and rollback plans.

faq.question: Which tools does it mention?
faq.answer: It references common tools such as DataDog, New Relic, OpenTelemetry, Grafana, k6, Gatling, Artillery, and Lighthouse CI.

faq.question: Can it create a continuous optimization process?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes performance budgets, regression testing, dashboards, alerts, SLOs, capacity planning, and review cycles.
